title: T1 Lex Primitiva — Universal Concept Algebra Tools
description: 15 axiomatic primitives forming a universal concept algebra. Each tool analyzes a concept through one primitive lens, returning structured decomposition with properties and failure modes. Proven in 216 tests across 70 properties. DISCLAIMER: This WebMCP configuration was developed by NexVigilant, LLC and is provided as a community resource. NexVigilant is not responsible for third-party use. Built by NexVigilant (https://nexvigilant.com) — Empowerment Through Vigilance.

tools.name: analyze-nothing
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Nothing (void) primitive. Detects absence, gaps, missing elements, and void states. Nothing is the identity element for existence — what is absent defines what is present.

parameters.name: concept
parameters.description: The concept, system, or domain to analyze for void/absence

tools.name: analyze-state
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the State primitive. Identifies observable states, transitions, and distinguishability. State is the variable before boundary fixes it — what can change.

parameters.name: system
parameters.description: The system or entity to analyze for state

parameters.name: observation
parameters.description: Optional: specific observation or context narrowing the state analysis

tools.name: analyze-boundary
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Boundary primitive. Identifies where things begin and end — the function that creates identity. Boundary is asymmetric: inside differs from outside.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity or system to analyze for boundaries

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Optional: the containing context or environment

tools.name: analyze-existence
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Existence primitive. Tests the conservation law: existence = boundary applied to the product of state and nothing. If any term vanishes, existence collapses.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The subject to test for existence

tools.name: analyze-causality
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Causality primitive. Maps cause-effect chains, temporal ordering, and derivation strength. Every function, every consequence.

parameters.name: cause
parameters.description: The proposed cause or antecedent

parameters.name: effect
parameters.description: The proposed effect or consequent

tools.name: analyze-comparison
tools.description: Analyze two concepts through the Comparison primitive. Computes symmetric difference — what is shared, what is unique to each. The universal primitive for relating any two things.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: First concept to compare

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second concept to compare

tools.name: analyze-quantity
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Quantity primitive. Identifies what is countable, measurable, and quantifiable. Built on the Peano construction: zero + successor.

parameters.name: concept
parameters.description: The concept to analyze for quantity

tools.name: analyze-sequence
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Sequence primitive. Identifies ordering, dependencies, and iteration patterns. What must come before what.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: The items, steps, or elements to analyze for sequence (comma-separated or description)

tools.name: analyze-mapping
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Mapping primitive. Identifies transformations between domains — what maps to what, what is shared, what gaps exist. The bridge between worlds.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source domain or concept

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target domain or concept

tools.name: analyze-recursion
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Recursion primitive. Detects self-reference, fixed points, and containment depth. Does the structure contain itself?

parameters.name: structure
parameters.description: The structure or concept to analyze for self-reference

tools.name: analyze-frequency
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Frequency primitive. Identifies rate, rhythm, repetition, and periodicity patterns. How often does something occur?

parameters.name: signal
parameters.description: The signal, pattern, or phenomenon to analyze for frequency

tools.name: analyze-persistence
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Persistence primitive. Identifies what endures, what mechanism preserves it, and its lifetime. State that survives.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity to analyze for persistence

tools.name: analyze-location
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Location primitive. Identifies address, reference, path, and reachability. Where is it in its domain?

parameters.name: reference
parameters.description: The entity or concept to locate

tools.name: analyze-irreversibility
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Irreversibility primitive. Identifies entropy direction, reversibility, and consequence permanence. Can it be undone?

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The action, process, or change to analyze for reversibility

tools.name: analyze-sum
tools.description: Analyze a concept through the Sum (disjoint union) primitive. Identifies variants, exhaustiveness, and selection. Which variant? Is the enumeration complete?

parameters.name: variants
parameters.description: The variants, options, or cases to analyze (comma-separated or description)
